exercises.name: Cable One Arm Curl
exercises.notes: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; grab the handle with a supinated (palm-up) grip using one hand.
Start with the working arm fully extended at your side and the elbow close to your torso; brace your core (abs) and maintain a neutral spine.
Keeping the upper arm stationary and the elbow fixed at your side, curl the handle upward by contracting the biceps until your forearm is near vertical or the knuckles approach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the biceps, then slowly lower the handle back to the start position under control to emphasize the eccentric phase.
Avoid swinging the torso or using the shoulder and delts to lift; if you need momentum, reduce the weight.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat with the opposite arm, maintaining the same posture and tempo for both sides.
exercises.description: Standing single-arm curl using a low cable pulley to load the biceps through the concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering; performed one arm at a time to emphasize unilateral strength and minimize momentum. Keep the elbow tucked to isolate the biceps and use a full range of motion.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Seated Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grasp an EZ curl bar with a narrow/neutral grip and lift it overhead.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and a neutral spine; grasp an EZ curl bar with a narrow/neutral grip and lift it overhead.
Hold the bar directly above your head with arms fully extended and elbows pointing forward (upper arms vertical and close to your ears).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar behind your head by flexing only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary and elbows tucked in.
Lower until your forearms are near your biceps or you feel a stretch in the triceps, avoiding excessive shoulder movement or flaring of the elbows.
Exhale and press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are fully straight overhead, maintaining control and avoiding locking aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps while keeping the torso stable, core engaged, and using a controlled tempo to prevent momentum.
exercises.description: Seated overhead triceps extension using an EZ curl bar to isolate the triceps by flexing and extending the elbows while keeping the upper arms stationary. Performed seated to reduce momentum and focus tension on the triceps.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Standing French Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
Brace your core and keep your chest up and spine neutral; avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Raise the bar overhead so your arms are straight, then slowly lower the bar behind your head by flexing only at the elbows until your forearms are near your triceps or just below ear level.
Keep your upper arms stationary and pointing up (elbows tucked in toward the head); do not let the elbows drift out or flare wide.
Pause briefly at the bottom with a controlled stretch, then press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (avoid locking out aggressively).
Breathe in as you lower the bar and exhale as you press it up. Maintain a firm grip and neutral wrist position to avoid strain.
Perform repetitions with controlled tempo and stop if you feel excessive elbow or shoulder pain; reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow position or a neutral spine.
exercises.description: Standing overhead French press using an EZ bar to isolate the triceps by flexing and extending the elbows with the bar lowered behind the head. The exercise is performed upright with the upper arms kept vertical and the core braced to prevent lumbar arching.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Alternate Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending the elbow, then extend the arm back up to the starting position.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and hold a dumbbell in one hand, raising it above your head with the elbow bent.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending the elbow, then extend the arm back up to the starting position.
Alternate arms for each repetition, keeping the core engaged and the movements controlled.
exercises.description: The Dumbbell Seated Alternate Overhead Triceps Extension targets the triceps by extending the arm overhead while seated. It helps in strengthening and toning the upper arms.

exercises.name: Cable Overhead Triceps Extension Straight Bar
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing away from the machine with feet staggered (one foot slightly forward for balance) and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, overhand grip.

Step 3: Raise the bar overhead and bend the elbows so the bar is positioned behind your head; keep upper arms close to your ears and elbows pointing upward (slight forward lean at the hips is acceptable).
Step 4: Brace your abs, keep a neutral spine, and stabilize your shoulders (minimize movement of the delts).
Step 5: Exhale and extend both elbows to press the bar upward until your arms are fully straight but not locked aggressively; squeeze the triceps at the top.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position behind your head with controlled tension (controlled eccentric), keeping the upper arms stationary.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining strict form; avoid flaring elbows out, arching the lower back, or using momentum from the shoulders.
Step 8: To finish safely, return the bar to the starting support or lower the weight before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Standing cable overhead triceps extension using a straight bar: perform controlled elbow extensions with the bar starting behind the head and finishing with arms straight overhead to isolate the triceps. Maintain a stable torso and stationary upper arms so the movement occurs at the elbows.

exercises.name: Cable Reverse Grip Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; grip the SZ bar with an underhand (palms up) reverse grip roughly shoulder-width apart.
Tuck your elbows close to your sides and keep your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and extend your elbows to push the bar down until your arms are nearly straight and triceps are fully contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid flaring the elbows.
Pause briefly at the bottom to feel the triceps contraction without locking the elbows or using torso momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the start position under control, returning to about a 90-degree elbow angle or slightly less while maintaining elbow position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement tempo controlled and avoiding shoulder or torso involvement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip to emphasize elbow extension and load the triceps. Perform controlled extensions and returns while keeping the upper arms stationary to isolate the triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Standing One Arm Triceps Pushdown (Overhand Grip)
exercises.notes: Push the handle down in a controlled motion until your arm is fully extended, then slowly return to the starting position.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the cable handle with an overhand grip, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Push the handle down in a controlled motion until your arm is fully extended, then slowly return to the starting position.
Keep your core engaged and avoid using momentum to perform the movement.
exercises.description: The Cable Standing One Arm Triceps Pushdown targets the triceps muscle, enhancing arm strength and definition. It involves pushing down a cable while maintaining an overhand grip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Cross Body Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
instructions: Start standing upright with feet hip-width apart, core braced and shoulders relaxed.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip, arm fully extended at your side and wrist neutral; the other hand can rest lightly on your hip.
Keep your upper arm and elbow close to your torso and stationary; do not let the elbow travel forward excessively.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell up across your body toward the opposite shoulder in a diagonal path, leading with the thumb while maintaining the neutral grip.
Pause and squeeze at the top for a moment, keeping the wrist straight and the torso stable (do not lean or swing the body).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control, fully extending the elbow but avoiding locking it harshly.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, then switch sides and perform the same steps with the opposite arm.
Maintain steady breathing and a controlled tempo throughout; reduce weight if you must swing or use momentum.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about shoulder height and load appropriate weight; step under the bar and place it across your upper traps (back-squat position), hands gripping the bar for stability.
Stand with the balls of your feet on a raised platform, plate, or step so your heels can drop below the platform; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward.
Unrack the bar and step clear of the rack, keeping your torso tall, chest up and core braced.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, keeping knees straight but not locked and avoiding forward lean.
Pause and squeeze the calves at the top for a second.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent—do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired reps, then carefully step back into the rack to re-rack the bar.
Common cues: keep a neutral spine, maintain steady tempo, breathe out on the lift and in on the lower, and use a spotter or safety pins if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raises are a weighted heel-raise exercise performed with a barbell across the upper back to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves). The movement raises the heels up onto the balls of the feet and then lowers back down for a full calf contraction and stretch.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and fasten it securely around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and fasten it securely around the working ankle.

Stand facing the machine, step back so the cable has light tension, hinge at the hips and lean forward, resting your forearms or hands on the cable frame or a support; keep a slight bend in the standing knee.
Set your spine neutral (no excessive rounding or arching) and brace your core (abs) to stabilize the trunk.
Initiate the movement by driving the working heel backward and slightly upward through the hip, extending the hip while keeping the knee softly bent and the pelvis square to the floor.
Squeeze the glute at the top of the movement and avoid rotating or tilting the pelvis; keep the torso stationary against the support.
Slowly control the cable as you return the leg to the start position until tension is maintained but the foot does not touch the floor (or returns lightly), maintaining core stability.
Breathe out during the hip extension and inhale during the return. Perform the desired number of reps, then switch sides.
Use a full, controlled range of motion with a weight that allows you to maintain a neutral spine and avoid momentum throughout the set.

The non-working leg remains on the floor for balance while the torso is braced on the machine.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.

exercises.name: Lever Sitting Toe Raise
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.

Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge and grasp the machine handles or sides of the bench for stability.
Start with your heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet, plantarflexing the ankles to raise your heels as high as possible while keeping the knees stationary.
Hold the top position for a brief pause, squeezing the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the start position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping a controlled tempo and maintaining upright torso and stable knee position throughout.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Squat Calf Raise on Leg Press Machine

Place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the footplate, feet about hip-width and toes pointing forward; knees should be bent (approximately 90°) throughout the exercise.
Unlock the machine safety if applicable and allow your heels to lower until you feel a stretch in the calves — this is the start position.
Exhale and contract the calves to raise your heels by pressing through the balls of your feet, lifting as high as possible without extending or locking the knees; pause 1 second at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back toward the start position under control until you feel a stretch in the calves; avoid bouncing.
Maintain a stable torso and fixed knee angle throughout the set so movement comes primarily from the ankle joint.
After completing the set, lock or re-engage the machine safety before releasing the handles and exiting the seat.
exercises.description: A seated, squat-style calf raise performed on a leg-press/lever machine where the user plantarflexes the ankles against the footplate to target the calf muscles while the hips and knees remain in a flexed (seated/squat) position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat with Calf Raise

instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes pointing slightly outward; hold a dumbbell vertically at your chest with both hands (goblet grip).
Brace your abs, keep a neutral spine and chest up, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees to descend into a squat.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or a comfortable depth), ensuring your knees track over your toes and your heels remain planted.
Drive through your heels to return to a standing position, fully extending the hips and squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement.
Without pausing, rise up onto the balls of your feet (calf raise) by contracting the calves, then lower the heels back down under control to flat feet.
Reset your core and posture, then repeat the squat-to-calf-raise sequence for the desired number of repetitions.

Start with the working knee bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and the foot dorsiflexed (toes up) so the pad contacts above the ankle.

Slowly return the leg to the start position under control, maintaining tension in the glute and hamstrings.
Complete the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat.
